title: acaena stangii
intro: Acaena stangii, also known as the blueberry or woolly cat's ear, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and woolly foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Acaena stangii include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, making it a potential treatment for minor skin irritations and injuries. Herbal preparations of Acaena stangii can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, which can be applied topically to affected areas.
